sections.paragraph:
ἀγαναϰτέω [etym. unclear]

—1. inward aspect ‘be upset about someth. that violates one’s sense of propriety’, be vexed, be distressed, be annoyed, Mt 20:24; 21:15; Mk 10:14; Lk 13:14.

—2. verbal aspect ‘express indignation’, Mk 14:4 ἀ. πρὸς ἑαυτούς, vehemently shared their displeasure.
